name: EfficientNet
training_approach: supervised training on ImageNet with AutoML-discovered base architecture
lineage_path: McCulloch-Pitts neuron -> Perceptron -> MLP -> LeNet -> AlexNet -> VGGNet -> ResNet -> EfficientNet
summary: EfficientNet introduced compound scaling, a principled method for uniformly scaling network depth, width, and input resolution using a fixed ratio discovered through neural architecture search. It achieved state-of-the-art ImageNet accuracy while being 8.4x smaller and 6.1x faster than the best existing CNNs, demonstrating that efficient model design through systematic scaling could outperform brute-force approaches. It represented the culmination of the CNN scaling paradigm before vision transformers emerged.
